== Notes for [[Item:Q100032|Q100032]] ==

Abraham ben Meir Ibn Ezra (Hebrew: אַבְרָהָם אִבְּן עֶזְרָא or ראב"ע‎; Arabic: ابن عزرا‎; also known as Abenezra or Aben Ezra, 1089–c.1167 [1][2]) was one of the most distinguished Jewish biblical commentators and philosophers of the Middle Ages. He was born in Tudela, Navarre, in northern Spain, one of the oldest and most important Jewish communities in Navarre, but the location of his death is uncertain: and for long it had been assumed that he died at Calahorra.

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
67051AD7B5BEB1EFBBFA9822EF79916C44A6
geni:6000000017139883079

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
